title: Largest Triangle
description:
A “terrain” is an $x$-monotone polygon defined by the points $p_1, \dots , p_n$ where each point $p_i$ has coordinates $(x_i , y_i)$, and the following three conditions hold:

  • $y_1 = y_n = 0$
  • $y_i > 0$ for $1 < i < n$
  • $x_i < x_{i+1}$ for $1 \le i < n$

Given a terrain defined by the points $p_1, \dots , p_n$, find the largest triangle that fits entirely within the terrain, and one of its three vertices is positioned at one of the terrain points $p_2$ through $p_{n-1}$.
input: The first line of input contains an integer $n$, representing the number of points in the terrain ($3 \le n \le 10^5$). The $i$th line in the following $n$ lines consists of two space-separated integers $x_i$ and $y_i$, representing the point $p_i$ of the terrain ($0 \le x_i , y_i \le 10^9$).
output: Print the area of the largest triangle contained within the terrain. Your output will be considered correct if its absolute or relative error is at most $10^{-6}$.
source: ICPC, Regionals, Asia West Continent, Iran, 2023 ICPC Asia Tehran Regional Contest
